purpose: Post-staging check of the stem-misstatement interpretation behind this batch's DM corrections. A true dedispersion error shifts every timing marker by the same Delta-DM; a scattering-lag artifact grows with the marker's sensitivity to the tau(nu) tail.

phase_coherence_dm_catalog.method: DM_phase-style phase-coherence statistic (manuscript adopted-DM analysis, analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2)

pulse_onset.method: sub-band 35%-of-peak leading-edge crossing vs K_DM nu^-2, 6 sub-bands, channel bootstrap (seed 20260707), display grid via plot_codetection_gallery.load_band at the adopted DM
pulse_onset.scattering_sensitivity: moderate (rise time scales with tau when scattering dominates)

peak_sharpness_scan.method: this batch's frequency-drift-scan.json (band-summed profile peak maximization)

verdict: REFUTED: the apparent Delta-DM is marker-dependent, increasing monotonically with scattering sensitivity (0.000 -> ~0.15 -> ~0.35), and the phase-coherence measurement excludes the applied corrections at >40 sigma. The corrections in this batch absorb scattering morphology into spurious dispersion; the archival stems are consistent with the actually-applied DMs. The uncorrected companion batch (2026-07-17-fig1-observed-peak-audit) displays the products at the adopted DM; the visual smear in isha/phineas CHIME is scattering, not residual sweep.
consequence: This candidate should not be promoted. The merged sharpness-scan audit remains valid as a measurement; its stem-misstatement interpretation and the derived corrections do not.
